channel.title: The SIP Lab
channel.description: Recent content on The SIP Lab

item.title: VoIP Explained #1: When Do You Need OpenSIPS or Kamailio? The Evolution of Softswitch Architecture
item.description:
The Simplest Architecture – The 1-Node Architecture

1-Node Architecture: 1 FreeSWITCH Node

In the simplest VoIP architecture, you only need to deploy a single FreeSWITCH server with a public IP address to start providing calling services.

Extensions register directly to the FreeSWITCH server, and the server connects directly to SIP trunks.

However, it doesn’t take long before you start encountering the following problems:

Security Issues

  • Direct Internet exposure
    FreeSWITCH is directly exposed to the public Internet, which inevitably means it will be attacked.
    While trunk connections can be protected with network policies, extension IP addresses are often dynamic.

item.title: About Me
item.description:
About Me

Hi, I’m Eddie Wang, a VoIP engineer and developer.

I specialize in VoIP technologies and have extensive experience working with SIP, OpenSIPS, Kamailio, and related real-time communication systems.

I’m also a long-time Vim user and a strong believer in open knowledge sharing.

  • Blog: https://wdd.js.org
  • WeChat ID: suguswang177

Subscribe

You can follow my latest posts via RSS:

https://wdd.js.org/index.xml
